title: cyperus lateriticus
intro: Cyperus lateriticus, also known as Tithonia diversifolia, is a flowering plant species native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia, characterized by its yellow flowers and palmate leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The health benefits of Cyperus lateriticus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a valuable remedy for conditions such as arthritis and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical creams, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
